instruction: Which of the formulas below could be a polynomial with all of the following properties: its only zeros are $x=-6,-2, 4$, it has $y$-intercept $y=6$, and its long-run behavior is $y \to-\infty$ as $x \to \pm \infty$? Select every formula that has all of these properties. [ANS] A. $y=-\frac{6}{192} (x+6)(x+2)(x-4)^2$  B. $y=-\frac{6}{96} (x+6)(x+2)^2(x-4)$  C. $y=-6x(x+6)(x+2)(x-4)$  D. $y=-\frac{6}{48} (x+6)(x+2)(x-4)$  E. $y=-\frac{6}{288} (x+6)^2(x+2)(x-4)$  F. $y=-\frac{6}{10368} (x+6)^4(x+2)(x-4)$  G. $y=-\frac{6}{288} (x-6)(x-2)(x+4)$
